apt: adjective. Suitable; appropriate; fit or fitted; suited.
cad: noun. A low-bred, presuming person; a mean, vulgar fellow, especially one that cannot be trusted with a lady.
cade: adjective. abandoned by its mother and reared by hand
cadet: noun. A student at a military school who is training to be an officer.
cap: noun. A close-fitting hat, either brimless or peaked.
cape: noun. A piece or point of land, extending beyond the adjacent coast into a sea or lake; a promontory; a headland.
caped: adjective. Wearing a cape or capes.

cede: verb. To give up; yield to another. [with to]
cee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter C / c.

deep: adjective. Extending, reaching or positioned far from a point of reference, especially downwards.
eat: verb. To ingest; to be ingested.
epact: noun. the time (number of days) by which a solar year exceeds twelve lunar months; it is used in the calculation of the date of Easter
eta: noun. The seventh letter of the Modern Greek alphabet, the eighth in Old Greek.
paced: verb. simple past and past participle of pace
pact: noun. An agreement; a compact; a covenant.
pad: noun. A flattened mass of anything soft, to sit or lie on.
pat: noun. The sound of a light slap or tap with a soft flat object, especially of a footstep.

pated: adjective. Having a pate or a particular type of pate (head)

pet: noun. An animal kept as a companion or otherwise for pleasure, rather than for some practical benefit or use.
tad: noun. A small amount; a little bit.
tae: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tariana.
tap: noun. A conical peg or pin used to close and open the hole or vent in a container.
